steps.explanation: The minimum recorded height is 42 feet, so no tree can be shorter than 42 feet: $t \geq 42$. The maximum is 57 feet, so no tree can be taller than 57 feet: $t \leq 57$.
Both constraints must hold simultaneously for every recorded value, giving the compound inequality $42 \leq t \leq 57$.

Evaluate each choice:
  • $t \leq 42$: Fails for any tree taller than 42 feet (e.g., $t = 50$). Eliminated.
  • $t \geq 57$: Fails for any tree shorter than 57 feet (e.g., $t = 42$). Eliminated.
  • $t \geq 99$: No recorded height reaches 99 feet. Fails for all values. Eliminated.
  • $42 \leq t \leq 57$: True for every value in the recorded range. That matches choice D — D is correct.
